mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings and equity valuations higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS percentage change and forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over any two-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market distress and impending economic weakness, leading to negative equity returns in the subsequent 20 days.
failure_condition: In months following a trigger event, the mean 20-day return is not negative, or is not significantly lower than non-trigger months at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) contract simultaneously, it indicates broad-based economic weakness that depresses corporate earnings and equity prices over the quarter ahead.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0 AND HOUST month-over-month growth < 0
failure_condition: When both series contract, the forward 60-day return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), positive payroll growth (PAYEMS) reinforces the expansion and supports higher equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Within the low unemployment regime, the sign of PAYEMS-forward return correlation is negative or not statistically different from zero over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to near-zero levels (below 0.25%), it signals extraordinary distress or recession fears, causing short-term negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: After trigger events, the average 5-day forward return is zero or positive, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals underlying economic strength that supports higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and forward 60-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises (CPIAUCSL increasing) while the fed funds rate remains low (FEDFUNDS below 0.25%), real interest rates decline, boosting equity valuations via the discount rate channel.
regime_hint: low nominal rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: In months when the trigger condition holds, the forward 20-day return is negative more often than positive, or the mean return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL above 280 index points), an increase in the fed funds rate signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Within the high inflation regime, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward 20-day returns is not negative, or the mean return after FEDFUNDS increases is not negative.
